misconceptionSummary: Students fill branches without checking that sibling branches sum to their parent node.

visuals.ruleText: At every branch point, the two child nodes must sum to the parent node
visuals.subText: Start with the root total and work outward — never guess a branch value

steps.narration: A frequency tree divides a total into categories at each branch. At every split, the two branches must add back to the node above. Fill in using addition and subtraction — never guess.
The root is one hundred and twenty. Boys is seventy, so girls must be fifty — the two branches sum to the root. Always check before moving further down the tree.
Work down each branch: boys who passed is forty-five, so boys who failed is seventy minus forty-five equals twenty-five. Similarly for girls. Read across the leaves to find totals for pass or fail.

visuals.label: Each branch pair sums to parent → work outward from root

steps.narration: Seventy-seven students passed. Fill the tree systematically from the root, checking each branch pair sums correctly before moving on.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: filling in a branch without checking it sums with its sibling to the parent. Every pair of sibling branches must add to their parent — verify at each split.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Every branch split must balance: left + right = parent. Fill one branch from given information, subtract to find the other. Check before moving to the next level.
